---
title: "Final Project"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-16"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
#Loading Data
airline_data <- read_csv("airline_route_profitability.csv")
glimpse(airline_data)
```

```{r}
head(airline_data)
```

```{r}
#Missing value check
colSums(is.na(airline_data))
airline_clean <- na.omit(airline_data)

```
```{r}
glimpse(airline_clean)
```

```{r}
#Average profit by season
airline_clean %>%
  group_by(Season) %>%
  summarize(avg_profit = mean(Profit))
```

```{r}
# Average profit by route category
airline_clean %>%
  group_by(Route_Category) %>%
  summarize(avg_profit = mean(Profit))
```


```{r}
#Average profit by demand level
airline_clean %>%
  group_by(Demand_Level) %>%
  summarize(avg_profit = mean(Profit))
```

```{r}
#Flight count by season
ggplot(data = airline_clean, aes(x = Season)) +
  geom_bar()

```
Low - slowest travel period
Normal - regular travel period
Peak - Not super busy, not super quiet, more than Normal.
Shoulder - busiest travel period

```{r}
#How profit spreads out across flights?

ggplot(airline_clean, aes(x = Profit)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 50000)
```
x-axis values are in dollars. 0e+00 = $0, 5e+05 = $500,000, 1e+06 = $1,000

```{r}
#Profit by Route Category
ggplot(airline_clean, aes(x = Route_Category, y = Profit)) +
  geom_boxplot()
```

```{r}
#Load Factor VS Profit
ggplot(airline_clean, aes(x = Load_Factor, y = Profit)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1/3)
#Load_Factor = Passengers/Aircraft Capacity
```
